body:
Everything White House for 4/14/2017. |

**FEATURED**

**WHITE HOUSE MEMO**
This is a season of hope for millions of Americans. This week, Jewish families celebrate Passover and retell the story of God’s deliverance of the Jewish people. This Easter Sunday, Christians celebrate the resurrection of Christ and the promise of eternal salvation. America is a Nation of believers. As long as we have faith in each other, and trust in God, we will succeed!

Bernie Sanders is heading to states Trump won to take the fight to the GOP. We're backing him up with a new ad campaign.
---
[****](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170414-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Team,
[](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170414-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")
Bernie Sanders is taking our fight to states with Republican-held Senate seats -- including Arizona, Nebraska, Nevada, and Utah -- during this week's Senate recess. He's even heading to Kentucky to confront Mitch McConnell.

He's challenging Republican senators in their own backyards to tell voters why they tried to take away healthcare from millions of Americans and why they're backing policies that would hurt working families.

Meanwhile, we're backing up his message with ads in key states that Trump won, showing Republican incumbent senators and potential challengers caught on tape backing the disastrous GOP health care bill.

[**Chip in $1 to our Recess Resistance Fund now to help fund our ads and all our efforts to take the fight to the GOP during recess.**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170414-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Republicans are dodging town halls this recess, trying to avoid tough questions from their constituents on health care.

But Democrats aren't playing it safe -- we're speaking directly to voters in the states that voted for Trump to highlight the flaws in the GOP's agenda and win over the people who have misgivings about Trump after witnessing his first few months in office.

We know that the resistance isn't just limited to a few blue states. After Trump's inauguration, we saw women's marches in every state in the country. We saw nationwide rallies on health care. And now, we're continuing to take the fight to Republican strongholds.

**Pitch in $1 to our Recess Resistance Fund and ensure we can hold Republicans accountable nationwide:**

[**http://dscc.org/take-the-fight-to-the-GOP**](https://secure.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/resistance-fund?refcode=DPE-20170414-Myers-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

Thanks,
Mindy

Hi there,
Donald Trump, Steve Bannon, and Sean Spicer have been in the White House for nearly 100 days. So far they've:
* Tried to defund Planned Parenthood
* Rammed through an extreme Supreme Court nominee
* Ripped away Internet privacy
* Attempted to take access to affordable health care away from millions

The first nearly 100 days have been hard, and we know that there are more challenges ahead. It won't be easy, but the best way to navigate this new administration is to make sure that we're in this together.
[**Please, join thousands of other Team Maggie members and fill out our very brief Trump administration approval poll.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd7a/2a380d31/159de8cd/fefc2a/3200896621/VEsF/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20170412_100days-approval-poll_nd?source=e20170414_100approval2_nd")

name: Sen. Patty Murray (via DSCC.org)
subject: Trump's hoping we ignore this:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Yesterday, Donald Trump signed legislation that will allow states to block funding for Planned Parenthood. Join me in fighting back.
---
[****](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e1d6/2b07114c/5f3fd12e/736c1843/1182087909/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/pp?source=DPE-20170414-Murray-ProsHV")
Team,

Yesterday, President Donald Trump signed legislation that will allow states to block funding for family planning clinics that also provide access to critical women's health care -- including Planned Parenthood.

This latest attack on women's health care is truly appalling, but unfortunately not surprising. And that's why we can't stop fighting.

[**Add your name right now if you're with me and Senate Democrats on this: stop the attacks on women's health.**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e1d6/2b07114c/5f3fd12e/736c1843/1182087909/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/pp?source=DPE-20170414-Murray-ProsHV")

From Trump's Supreme Court pick to the GOP attempts to roll back the progress we've made on health care -- it just doesn't end.

They want us to be quiet. They're hoping we ignore this, and all they have planned. But we won't back down.

I won't stop fighting for the millions of women in this country, for their access to their health care, for them to be able to make decisions for themselves and for their constitutionally protected right to make their own choices about their own bodies.

**Are you with me? Add your name right now to stand with me and Senate Democrats to defend access to women's health care:**

[**http://dscc.org/protect-womens-health-care**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e1d6/2b07114c/5f3fd12e/736c1843/1182087909/VEsF/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/pp?source=DPE-20170414-Murray-ProsHV")

Thank you,

Senator Patty Murray

| _**House Republicans**_
**SB7 Heard Before House Health and Human Services Committee**
Senate Bill 7 was introduced in the Health and Human Services and Elderly Affairs committee on Wednesday. SB7 reforms the eligibility for food stamps by strengthening the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) and establishes the Granite Workforce pilot program. Sponsors of the bill say that the food stamp program has outpaced job growth, and over the last few years, it has grown exponentially. This bill is to reform the abuse that takes place, including the millions of dollars in back child support that is owed to families. Individuals receiving food stamps, who have failed to pay their child support, will work with the state to ensure they’re supporting their children before receiving benefits. It also includes an asset test in order to identify those who don’t need state assistance. SB7 will also require able-bodied adults to seek employment to raise them out of poverty, raise incomes, and ensure SNAP benefits are being used to assist those who truly need it. By re-purposing funds from Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) some common barriers to employment would be eliminated. TANF dollars would go towards providing job training, child care, and transportation in order to assist healthy adults return to work and help families transition off welfare. SB7 also includes important safety valves in the bill. At any time if the Department of Health and Human Services see that any of the provisions in this bill are adversely impacting NH citizens, they are able to petition the Health and Human Services Oversight Committee to go with a higher gross income standard. This is meant to be a short term program to get child care, job training, and placement. This bill does two things that is promoted in our Republican agenda: reforms entitlement programs and promotes individual responsibility.

**Morse, Bradley urge House Committee support for** **cross border drug interdiction efforts**
Concord, NH - Today, the House Criminal Justice and Public Safety committee heard SB 131, a bill to establish a cross border drug interdiction program.
This legislation outlines program that would use Fiscal Year ’17 funds to add five state troopers to work with Massachusetts officials targeting drugs and dealers at the border. This bill also adds $2.4 million to expand the successful Granite Hammer Program, allowing more communities to take advantage of drug interdiction efforts.
Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem) and Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statements after the hearing: “New Hampshire’s opioid crisis continued to be a major priority for the Senate and this legislation serves to target drug dealers, cutting the supply of deadly drugs, including heroin and fentanyl, from coming into this state,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem).
“Adding five additional troopers who will work directly with Massachusetts police and officials will make better use of our resources and critical information that leads to greater prevention of drugs crossing the border.”
“We realize that curbing this crisis requires a multifaceted approach providing programs and resources for prevention, treatment and recovery, but we should also be providing law enforcement with the tools they need to stop illegal drug activity before it reaches our communities,” added Morse.
“This legislation also serves to expand the Granite Hammer program which has shown considerable success in removing drug dealers from our streets, limiting the supply of heroin and fentanyl in our communities. It is programs like Granite Hammer that have a record of success where we continue to focus our efforts,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro).
“I want to thank Governor Sununu for his dedication to resolving the opioid crisis and for his leadership in bringing New Hampshire and Massachusetts officials together to work on cutting the supply of deadly drugs. I encourage the House to support adding troopers for cross border interdiction and expanding successful Granite Hammer program,” said Bradley.

This week's spotlight shines on the NHGOP Secretary, Jane Lane. She was nominated by RNC National Committeewoman Juliana Bergeron who describes Jane as "a Republican institution". Jane is in her second term as the NHGOP Secretary and also currently serves as Secretary of Cheshire County GOP. She served as a County Commissioner, ran for the State Senate, was President of the NH Federation of Republican Women and was the VP of the National Federation of Republican Women. The New Hampshire GOP is grateful for Jane's continued service and friendship! Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans SB7 Heard Before House Health and Human Services Committee Senate Bill 7 was introduced in the Health and Human Services and Elderly Affairs committee on Wednesday. SB7 reforms the eligibility for food stamps by strengthening the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) and establishes the Granite Workforce pilot program. Sponsors of the bill say that the food stamp program has outpaced job growth, and over the last few years, it has grown exponentially. This bill is to reform the abuse that takes place, including the millions of dollars in back child support that is owed to families. Individuals receiving food stamps, who have failed to pay their child support, will work with the state to ensure they’re supporting their children before receiving benefits. It also includes an asset test in order to identify those who don’t need state assistance. SB7 will also require able-bodied adults to seek employment to raise them out of poverty, raise incomes, and ensure SNAP benefits are being used to assist those who truly need it. By re-purposing funds from Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) some common barriers to employment would be eliminated. TANF dollars would go towards providing job training, child care, and transportation in order to assist healthy adults return to work and help families transition off welfare. SB7 also includes important safety valves in the bill. At any time if the Department of Health and Human Services see that any of the provisions in this bill are adversely impacting NH citizens, they are able to petition the Health and Human Services Oversight Committee to go with a higher gross income standard. This is meant to be a short term program to get child care, job training, and placement. This bill does two things that is promoted in our Republican agenda: reforms entitlement programs and promotes individual responsibility. | | Senate Republicans \------ Morse, Bradley urge House Committee support for cross border drug interdiction efforts Concord, NH - Today, the House Criminal Justice and Public Safety committee heard SB 131, a bill to establish a cross border drug interdiction program. This legislation outlines program that would use Fiscal Year ’17 funds to add five state troopers to work with Massachusetts officials targeting drugs and dealers at the border. This bill also adds $2.4 million to expand the successful Granite Hammer Program, allowing more communities to take advantage of drug interdiction efforts. Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem) and Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro) issued the following statements after the hearing: “New Hampshire’s opioid crisis continued to be a major priority for the Senate and this legislation serves to target drug dealers, cutting the supply of deadly drugs, including heroin and fentanyl, from coming into this state,” said Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem). “Adding five additional troopers who will work directly with Massachusetts police and officials will make better use of our resources and critical information that leads to greater prevention of drugs crossing the border.” “We realize that curbing this crisis requires a multifaceted approach providing programs and resources for prevention, treatment and recovery, but we should also be providing law enforcement with the tools they need to stop illegal drug activity before it reaches our communities,” added Morse. “This legislation also serves to expand the Granite Hammer program which has shown considerable success in removing drug dealers from our streets, limiting the supply of heroin and fentanyl in our communities. It is programs like Granite Hammer that have a record of success where we continue to focus our efforts,” said Senate Majority Leader Jeb Bradley (R-Wolfeboro). “I want to thank Governor Sununu for his dedication to resolving the opioid crisis and for his leadership in bringing New Hampshire and Massachusetts officials together to work on cutting the supply of deadly drugs. I encourage the House to support adding troopers for cross border interdiction and expanding successful Granite Hammer program,” said Bradley. | | Empowering Republican Women! | ---|---|--- A message from SBA Administrator Linda McMahon on Women's Empowerment. name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: March for Science: Join Us For Bagels, Coffee and Conversation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
It's been just 83 days since Donald Trump took office and he keeps confirming our worst fears. From dangerously erratic foreign policy moves to disastrous health care proposals and reckless budget cuts, this Administration seems hell bent on reversing much of the progress we have made. They have coined terms like "alternative facts" to fit their alternative reality, and tried to turn fake news into real news.

But the good news is that the American people are fighting back to protect our country and her values. The day after the inauguration millions of Americans turned out for the Women's Marches in Washington and around the country. Since then, people have flocked to Town Hall meetings and flooded Congress with phone calls and emails. And we will keep mobilizing until we defeat the reckless Trump agenda so we can focus on advancing the common good.

The March for Science on Earth Day -- Saturday, April 22nd -- will be an important opportunity to demonstrate that 'We the People' will not stand for a President who seeks to bury the truth and has turned the White House into an evidence-free zone. [**On the morning of the Science March, I will be teaming up with the Council for a Livable World and community leaders to host a breakfast conversation over bagels and coffee at the Stuart Mott House on Capitol Hill. Hope you can join us.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91be7/70cdc2ba/4756c443/397f930/1471075854/VEsH/ "http://action.vanhollen.org/page/s/join-us-on-april-22-?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+On+the+morning+of+the+Science+March+I+wi&utm_campaign=em041417&source=em041417")

The Mott House is located at 122 Maryland Ave NE (a short walk from Union Station) and is a good launch point for the march. clean_body: | It's been just 83 days since Donald Trump took office and he keeps confirming our worst fears. From dangerously erratic foreign policy moves to disastrous health care proposals and reckless budget cuts, this Administration seems hell bent on reversing much of the progress we have made. They have coined terms like "alternative facts" to fit their alternative reality, and tried to turn fake news into real news. But the good news is that the American people are fighting back to protect our country and her values. The day after the inauguration millions of Americans turned out for the Women's Marches in Washington and around the country. Since then, people have flocked to Town Hall meetings and flooded Congress with phone calls and emails. And we will keep mobilizing until we defeat the reckless Trump agenda so we can focus on advancing the common good. The March for Science on Earth Day -- Saturday, April 22nd -- will be an important opportunity to demonstrate that 'We the People' will not stand for a President who seeks to bury the truth and has turned the White House into an evidence-free zone. name: OurRevolution.com
subject: Join us for the filming of the national livestream and the first Our Rev Maryland Statewide Meeting!
body:
Our Revolution groups and supporters nationwide are gathering to host State of the Revolution
#
Friends,
Join activists and supporters from every corner of the state at Our Revolution Maryland's first statewide meeting.
**[Please click here to RSVP to the statewide Our Revolution Maryland meeting](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0ae7/43f17890/ddc047b6/6203d9a8/3953405832/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/townhall/gpghsz")**

**Sunday, April 23 3:30 - 4:30 PM**
Tommy Douglas Conference Center
10000 New Hampshire Ave
Silver Spring, MD 20903
**During the meeting, you'll:**

* Hear reports from leaders in all Maryland counties
* Get Our Revolution Maryland's assessment of the 2017 Maryland legislative session
* Discuss next steps for Maryland

Immediately following the meeting, join Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen for the live recording of the State of the Revolution livestream.
**[Please click here to RSVP to the statewide Our Revolution Maryland meeting](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0ae7/43f17890/ddc047b6/6203d9a8/3953405832/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/townhall/gpghsz")**
Solidarity,
OR Maryland
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: OurRevolution.com
Date: Thu, Apr 13, 2017 at 8:42 PM
Subject: Join us for the filming of the State of the Revolution livestream, Live in Silver Spring!
Sisters and brothers,
Our Revolution groups and supporters nationwide are gathering to host State of the Revolution livestream events to celebrate how far we have come and outline the journey ahead. The political revolution is just beginning, and communities like yours will be on the frontlines of the fight for the progressive agenda that has brought us together, and **Our Revolution Maryland, a state-wide organizing committee, is excited to announce that we're hosting the event!**
**On Sunday, April 23, join local supporters for the live filming of the State of the Revolution livestream event in Silver Spring. Be a part of the mass people-powered movement to transform American politics.**
**Tommy Douglas Conference Center**
Sunday, April 23 4:30 - 6:00 PM
10000 New Hampshire Ave
Silver Spring, MD 20903
**[RSVP TO ATTEND»](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0ae7/43f17890/ddc047b6/6203d9a9/3953405832/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/event/detail/townhall/gpghsk")**

The livestream will be headlined by former Ohio State Senator Nina Turner, former NAACP President and CEO Ben Jealous, former Nevada State Assemblywoman Lucy Flores, Massachusetts State Representative Mike Connolly and Our Revolution Board Chairman Larry Cohen.

Together, we'll:
* Celebrate the victories you and supporters like you have made possible both locally and nationally
* Outline the integral role you and your community will play in continuing the movement
* Introduce the tools, resources, and campaigns you and your group can use to take immediate action after the livestream

Our movement has the potential to be the largest force for progressive political action this country has ever seen -- but it will take all of us getting involved at the grassroots for it to be successful. **Will you join the live filming of the State of the Revolution livestream on April 23 with progressives in Silver Spring?**
_" You are standing up and fighting back, and that is what the political revolution is about." \- Bernie Sanders_
In solidarity,
Our Revolution Maryland

PAID FOR BY OUR REVOLUTION
603 2ND ST, NE
WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. Our Revolution is a 501(c)(4) organization. Donations to Our Revolution are not deductible as charitable contributions for Federal income tax purposes. All donations are made to support Our Revolution's general mission and are not designated for any specific activity.
603 2nd St NE, Washington, DC 20002

If you need to update or change your information or email address, click here to [update your info](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0ae7/43f17890/ddc047b6/6203d956/3953405832/VEsC/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/information-update?source=email_footer"). Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123 "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0ae7/43f17890/ddc047b6/6203d957/3953405832/VEsD/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0ae7/43f17890/ddc047b6/6203d954/3953405832/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").

name: Robert Silver
subject: I'm sure you don't want to miss out on this
body:
You could join Tom and Bernie in Las Vegas next weekend. **Pitch in $3 or more to be automatically entered for a chance to join Tom and Bernie on the Come Together and Fight Back Tour in Las Vegas!

** **[ENTER](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb2d/2274431766/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Tom-and-Bernie-in-Vegas-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ENTER&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")**
Hi. My name is Robert Silver. I'm one of the millions of Democrats across the country who have supported the DNC and our party's vision for America.

Last month, I received an email about a contest to meet Senator Elizabeth Warren in Washington. I figured that the odds of winning were probably small, but the email also mentioned how important it was for me to support our party.

So, I made a contribution to help spread the Democratic Party's message across our nation -- easy to do with a few clicks -- and entered the contest. If I don't enter, I thought, I can't win -- and meeting Senator Warren had been a long-time goal for me.

Well, the odds turned out in my favor!

I got a phone call one morning saying I'd won, and the next day I was shaking hands with Elizabeth Warren, Tom Perez, and Keith Ellison! My visit was an experience that I'll always remember.

And it was remarkable to see the results of my $25 donation in action, helping Democrats reach out to people in every state and communicate our shared values.

That's why I wanted to let you know about the DNC's latest opportunity: **[the chance to join Tom Perez and Bernie Sanders on the Come Together and Fight Back Tour in Las Vegas.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb2a/2274431766/VEsE/ "https://my.democrats.org/Tom-and-Bernie-in-Vegas-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+the+chance+to+join+Tom+Perez+and+Bernie+&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")**

If you win, the DNC will handle all of the logistics: your flight, hotel stay, and tickets to see Bernie and Tom talk about how we can unite and organize our party.

So take your shot! Trust me, it can happen to you -- but only if you enter.

**If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately.

[ QUICK DONATE: $3](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb2b/2274431766/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/come-together-and-fight-back-contest?express_lane=true&amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+QUICK+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $10](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb28/2274431766/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/come-together-and-fight-back-contest?express_lane=true&amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+QUICK+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $25](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb29/2274431766/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/come-together-and-fight-back-contest?express_lane=true&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+QUICK+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $50](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb36/2274431766/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/come-together-and-fight-back-contest?express_lane=true&amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+QUICK+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")
[QUICK DONATE: $100](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb37/2274431766/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/come-together-and-fight-back-contest?express_lane=true&amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=7+-+QUICK+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb34/2274431766/VEsO/ "https://my.democrats.org/Tom-and-Bernie-in-Vegas-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=8+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd")
** Good luck -- and, hopefully, you too will get that phone call and get to join Tom Perez and Bernie Sanders!

All the best,

Robert Silver

---

No purchase, payment, or contribution necessary to enter or win. Contributing will not improve chances of winning. Void where prohibited. Entries must be received by 4/16/17. [Enter by contributing here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb35/2274431766/VEsP/ "http://my.democrats.org/tour-contest?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=9+-+Enter+by+contributing+here&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd"), or click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb32/2274431766/VEsHBQ/ "http://my.democrats.org/tour-contest-signup?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=10+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd") to enter without contributing. One (1) winner will receive the following prize package: round-trip tickets for winner and one guest from within the fifty U.S. States, DC, or Puerto Rico to Las Vegas, NV; one (1) night's hotel accommodations for winner and one guest; the opportunity for winner and winner's guest to attend a DNC event on 4/22/17 (approximate retail value of all prizes $3,000). Odds of winning depend on number of entries received. Promotion open only to U.S. citizens, or lawful permanent U.S. residents who are legal residents of 50 United States, District of Columbia and Puerto Rico and 18 or older (or age of majority under applicable law). Promotion subject to [Official Rules](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb33/2274431766/VEsHBA/ "http://my.democrats.org/tour-contest-rules?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=11+-+Official+Rules&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd") and additional restrictions on eligibility. Sponsor: Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington, DC 20003.

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Contributions or gifts to the Democratic National Committee are not tax deductible.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb30/2274431766/VEsHBw/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=12+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb31/2274431766/VEsHBg/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=13+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd"). Click [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb3e/2274431766/VEsHAQ/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=14+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052c9af/4067b0a/2ea728bd2/7e72bb3f/2274431766/VEsHAA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=15+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20170414_rs_nd&source=em_20170414_rs_nd&refcode=em_20170414_rs_nd"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

name: Leigh K. Warren, EMILY's List
subject: Last chance: Help re-elect our ⑨ Senate women
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7eab/24c69fd9/ef391bce/18e2c077/1420341378/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFE415N_digital_email_20170414_SenLastChance_inact&source=WQEFE415N_digital_email_20170414_SenLastChance_inact")
---

Peter,

Today is the final day in our week-long hustle to raise $100,000 to defend our nine Senate women who are up for re-election next year.

2018 is going to be a huge election year no matter what, and we'll be a big part of it.

Midterm elections are typically good for the party not in power, but Republicans are ready to do everything they can to defeat our Senate champions.

**[Today's the last day to hit our goal. Donate $3 to help defend our Senate women and grow their ranks in 2018.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7eab/24c69fd9/ef391bce/18e2c070/1420341378/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170410_email_SenateGoal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Todays+the+last+day+to+hit+our+goal+Dona&utm_campaign=WQEFE415N_digital_email_20170414_SenLastChance_inact&source=WQEFE415N_digital_email_20170414_SenLastChance_inact")**

Republicans only have to defend a handful of Senate seats next year. Democrats have to defend more than 20, and nine of our 15 EMILY's List women are up for re-election.

These senators have been doing everything they can to protect choice: fighting Republicans' shameful plan to take health insurance away from 24 million Americans and unanimously voting no on anti-choice Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch just last week.

Now it's our turn to defend these pro-choice champions.

**Donate $3 before midnight tonight to fight for the Senate women who fight for us every day:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Defend-our-Senators](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c7eab/24c69fd9/ef391bce/18e2c071/1420341378/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20170410_email_SenateGoal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgDefendourSenator&utm_campaign=WQEFE415N_digital_email_20170414_SenLastChance_inact&source=WQEFE415N_digital_email_20170414_SenLastChance_inact")**

Thanks,

clean_body: We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | "" Peter, Today is the final day in our week-long hustle to raise $100,000 to defend our nine Senate women who are up for re-election next year. 2018 is going to be a huge election year no matter what, and we'll be a big part of it. Midterm elections are typically good for the party not in power, but Republicans are ready to do everything they can to defeat our Senate champions. "" Republicans only have to defend a handful of Senate seats next year. Democrats have to defend more than 20, and nine of our 15 EMILY's List women are up for re-election. These senators have been doing everything they can to protect choice: fighting Republicans' shameful plan to take health insurance away from 24 million Americans and unanimously voting no on anti-choice Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch just last week.
